name: Rice-Stuffed Artichokes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the stems and loose outer leaves from the artichokes. Cut off 1-inch from the tops.
2. Snip off the sharp leaf tips. Brush the cut edges with lemon juice.
3. Place the artichokes and water in a casserole dish. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
4. Micro-cook, covered, on full power for 7 to 9 minutes or until tender, rotating the casserole a half-turn after 4 minutes. Let stand, covered, while preparing stuffing.
5. For stuffing, stir together shredded carrot, sliced green onion, butter (or margarine), and crushed sage in a small nonmetal bowl. Micro-cook, covered, on full power for 2 to 3 1/2 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring once.
6. Stir the vegetable mixture with cooked rice. Drain the artichokes.
7. Remove the center leaves and chokes from the artichokes. Spread the artichoke leaves slightly. Spoon the rice stuffing into the center of each artichoke and behind each large leaf.
8. Return the artichokes to the casserole dish. Cover with vented clear plastic wrap.
9. Micro-cook, covered, on full power for 2 to 3 minutes or until stuffing is hot and bases of artichokes are fork-tender, rotating the casserole a half-turn every minute.
10. Let stand, covered, while preparing sauce.
11. For sauce, stir together chicken broth, lemon juice, cornstarch, and white pepper in a 2-cup measure.
12. Micro-cook, uncovered, on full power for 2 to 3 minutes or until thickened and bubbly, stirring every 30 seconds.
13. Stir half of the hot sauce into the beaten egg yolk. Return all to the 2-cup measure. Micro-cook, uncovered, for 30 seconds.
14. Transfer stuffed artichokes to a warm serving platter. Pour sauce around the artichokes.
